card.title: Still need help?

forgot.title: Forgot your password?

forgot.successBody: If an account exists for that email, we've sent a reset link. Check your inbox (and spam folder) within the next few minutes. The link expires in 15 minutes.

reset.successBody: Your password has been changed. You can sign in with the new one now.

partnerCommissions.title: Commissions
partnerCommissions.subtitle: Earnings generated from your referrals.
partnerCommissions.noCommissions: No commissions yet. Commissions are created when your referrals subscribe.

explainer.title: How you get paid
explainer.section1: When a customer subscribes, your commission is locked for 30 days. If the customer refunds during that window, the commission gets reversed and you don't see it. After 30 days, the commission becomes eligible for payout. Recurring renewals from existing customers skip the hold.
explainer.section2: Eligible commissions are batched and paid automatically twice a month. If the 1st or 15th lands on a weekend, the payout fires the next business day. Each commission's exact payout date is shown in the "Pays on" column above.
explainer.section3Title: 3. Stripe processing fees come out of your payout
explainer.section3Intro: We pay you via Stripe direct deposit to your bank. Stripe charges two fees that are deducted from each payout:
explainer.section3Bullet1: **$2 / month** — Stripe Connect Express monthly fee, billed once per month on your first payout (only on months you actually receive a payout).
explainer.section3Bullet2: **~$0 standard ACH** (1–3 business day settlement) or **1.5% (min $0.50) instant** if you opt for same-day deposit.
explainer.exampleStripeFee: − Stripe Connect monthly fee (first payout this month)
explainer.exampleNote: The $2 monthly fee only hits once per month — every additional payout the same month is fee-free (or just the transfer fee if you pick instant).
explainer.minBody: You need at least **$50** in approved commissions for a payout cycle to fire. If your eligible balance is below the minimum on a 1st or 15th, it rolls into the next cycle.

partnerPayouts.title: Payouts
partnerPayouts.subtitle: Request payment of your approved commission balance.
partnerPayouts.needApproved: You need approved commissions before requesting a payout.
partnerPayouts.needConnect: Connect your Stripe payout account before requesting a payout.

partnerProfile.subtitle: Your partner account details and payout preferences.
partnerProfile.noAccount: No partner account found. Apply from the Dashboard.

partnerDashboard.commissionLine: You earn {pct}% recurring commission on every active subscription you refer.
partnerDashboard.applyDesc: Earn commissions by referring customers to MyOrbisVoice. Once approved, you'll get a unique referral link and real-time tracking.
partnerDashboard.pendingDesc: We'll notify you by email once your partner account is approved. You won't be able to track conversions until then.

checklist.title: What you need to get paid
checklist.desc: Complete these steps to start receiving commissions. You can earn referrals before completing them — payment just won't process until everything is checked.
checklist.activeDoneDetail: You can start sharing your referral link.
checklist.payoutTodoDetail: Connect your bank securely through Stripe to start receiving commissions.
checklist.payoutPendingDetail: Stripe is reviewing your details. We'll notify you when payouts are enabled.
checklist.taxDoneDetail: On file with Stripe — they'll mail you a 1099-NEC at year-end if you earn $600+.
checklist.taxTodoDetail: Collected automatically when you connect your payout account.
checklist.minTitle: Earn at least ${amount} in approved commissions
checklist.minDoneDetail: Minimum payout balance reached. Next payout fires on the 1st or 15th.
checklist.minTodoDetail: Currently ${current} of ${target} needed. Commissions clear after a 30-day hold and pay on the 1st or 15th of each month.
checklist.minTodoNextPayoutDetail: Currently ${current} of ${target} needed. Next scheduled payout: {date}.

partnerDashboard.noCommissions: No commissions yet. Your earnings will appear here once your referrals subscribe.

tenantAppointments.title: Appointments
tenantAppointments.subtitle: Bookings created by your voice agents.

empty.body: Connect your Google account and activate a channel. Appointments booked by agents will appear here.

tenantCampaigns.title: Campaigns
tenantCampaigns.subtitle: Automated voice follow-ups, confirmations, and outreach — triggered by contact tags.

howItWorks.title: How Campaigns Work
howItWorks.step1Body: Pick a pre-built campaign that fits your business. Each template includes a prompt, trigger tag, delay, and retry schedule.
howItWorks.step2Body: Click "+ Add to My Campaigns" to copy the template into your account. Customize the prompt and settings to match your tone.
howItWorks.step3Body: Toggle the campaign on. It will only fire calls once it is active — inactive campaigns are saved but never triggered.
howItWorks.step4Body: When a contact receives the campaign's trigger tag (manually or via a call outcome), a follow-up call is automatically scheduled.

library.searchPlaceholder: Search templates by name, description, or tag…
library.addedToast: "{name}" added to My Campaigns — customize and activate it there.

mine.emptyHint: Add one from the Template Library or create custom.
mine.selectPrompt: Select a campaign to edit or create a new one.
mine.deleteConfirm: Delete this campaign? Existing enrollments will also be removed.

validation.nameAndTagRequired: Name and trigger tag are required.
validation.atLeastOneChannel: Enable at least one channel (voice, SMS, email, or WhatsApp).
validation.voicePromptRequired: Voice prompt is required when voice is enabled.
validation.smsBodyRequired: SMS body is required when SMS is enabled.
validation.emailFieldsRequired: Email subject and body are required when email is enabled.

form.name: Campaign name
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this campaign does
form.channelsHint: Pick which channels this campaign dispatches through. Each enabled channel sends independently when the trigger fires.
form.triggerTagHint: The contact tag that starts this campaign automatically.
form.voicePromptHint: What the agent says on the call. Be specific about purpose and tone. Available variables: {firstName}, {fullName}, {businessName}, {businessPhone}, {appointmentDate}, {appointmentTime}.
form.voicePromptPlaceholder: You are calling to follow up on the contact's recent visit…
form.smsBodyHint: Plain text message. Use {firstName}, {businessName}, etc. {count}/1600 characters.
form.smsBodyPlaceholder: Hi {firstName}, this is {businessName} confirming your appointment. Reply STOP to opt out.
form.emailBodyHint: HTML allowed. Same variables as the subject.
form.emailBodyPlaceholder:
Hi {firstName},

We're looking forward to seeing you on {appointmentDate} at {appointmentTime}.

— The {businessName} team
form.whatsappBodyHint: WhatsApp dispatch is coming soon — content saved for when it launches.
form.whatsappBodyPlaceholder: Hi {firstName}! Your appointment with {businessName} is confirmed for {appointmentDate} at {appointmentTime}.
form.delayHoursHint: How long after the tag is applied before calling.
form.maxRetriesHint: How many times to retry if no answer.

tooltips.triggerTag: When a contact gets this tag (manually OR auto-applied by call outcomes like BOOKED, MISSED_CALL, CALLBACK_REQUESTED, INFO_REQUEST, QUALIFIED_LEAD), the campaign fires for them on every channel you've enabled below. Use lowercase-with-dashes — spaces auto-convert.
tooltips.voicePrompt: What the AI agent says when it calls. Be specific: who's calling, why, what action you want from them. Available variables auto-fill: {firstName}, {fullName}, {businessName}, {businessPhone}, {appointmentDate}, {appointmentTime}. The agent voice + business identity come from your Business DNA — don't repeat them here.
tooltips.delayHours: Time between the tag being applied and the campaign firing. 0 = right away. For example, 24 sends a day-after follow-up, 168 sends a week-after re-engagement. Counts from when the tag was applied, not from the original event (call, booking, etc.).
tooltips.maxRetries: If the channel attempt fails (no answer on voice, undelivered SMS, bounced email), how many MORE attempts before giving up. 0 = single attempt, no retries. 3 = original try plus 3 retries (4 total attempts).
tooltips.retryInterval: Hours between retry attempts. Counted from the LAST attempt, not from the original tag. Example: tag at 9am, delayHours=0, retryInterval=4, maxRetries=2 → first attempt 9am, retry 1 at 1pm, retry 2 at 5pm. Stops once any attempt succeeds.

businessHours.title: Business hours
businessHours.description: Calls outside these hours trigger the after-hours behaviour above

afterHours.title: After-hours
afterHours.behaviourTooltip: What happens when a call comes in outside the business hours you set below. Voicemail, forward to a person, or just answer normally with the AI.
afterHours.forwardTooltip: The phone where after-hours calls are sent. Usually a personal mobile or an answering service.
afterHours.forwardHelp: Calls outside business hours are forwarded here

voicePicker.label: Agent voice
voicePicker.tooltip: The voice your AI agent uses on every call. Pick one that matches your brand — you can change it anytime; takes effect on the next call.

agentSpeaksFirst.label: Agent speaks first
agentSpeaksFirst.description: Agent delivers the opening greeting immediately when the session starts, without waiting for the caller to speak.

greetingMode.label: Greeting mode
greetingMode.tooltip: A short word or phrase describing the tone of your agent's opening — passed into the prompt as guidance.
greetingMode.help: Sets the tone of the agent's opening message

escalation.modeTooltip: When the agent decides a caller needs a human (e.g. complex complaint, refund request), this controls how it hands off.
escalation.transferTooltip: The phone number the AI dials and bridges the caller to when escalating. Should be a number that's reliably answered during business hours.
escalation.transferHelp: The agent dials this number when escalating to a human

contactPicker.emptyContacts: No contacts with phone numbers. Add them in Contacts first.

phoneNumbers.title: Phone Numbers

tenantAgents.title: Agents
tenantAgents.subtitle: Configure each AI agent role for your workspace.
tenantAgents.selectAgent: Select an agent to configure it

ORCHESTRATOR.name: Orchestrator
ORCHESTRATOR.description: Coordinates all other agents and manages handoffs

SECRETARY.name: Secretary
SECRETARY.description: Manages schedule, notes, and admin tasks

displayName.label: Display name
displayName.tooltip: The name your agent uses when introducing itself on calls (e.g. 'Hi, this is Aria from Acme'). Pick something friendly and easy to hear over the phone.

modelProvider.label: Model provider
modelProvider.tooltip: Which AI provider routes this agent's reasoning. Default 'openai' — only change if you know what you're doing.

modelName.label: Model name
modelName.tooltip: The specific model used for this agent's text reasoning (summaries, follow-ups, decisions). Voice itself is always Gemini Live, set on the channel.

templates.title: Agent templates
templates.subtitle: Pick a pre-built agent personality to apply to your workspace. You can customize the prompt afterward.
templates.applied: Template applied. Your agent is updated.

tenantBusinessDna.title: Business DNA
tenantBusinessDna.subtitle: Versioned knowledge base that your AI agents draw on in every conversation.
tenantBusinessDna.emptyStateTitle: Select a version or create a new draft
tenantBusinessDna.readOnlyBanner: This version is currently active and read-only. Create a new draft to make changes.
tenantBusinessDna.jsonInvalidObject: Top-level value must be a JSON object.

confirm.discardChanges: You have unsaved changes. Discard them?
confirm.discardForNewDraft: You have unsaved changes. Discard them and create a new draft?
confirm.saveBeforePublish: You have unsaved changes. Save them before publishing? Cancel to abort.

toast.published: Business DNA published and now active.

identity.label: Identity
identity.description: Your business name, mission, brand voice, and what you do at a glance. The agent uses this to introduce itself and frame every conversation.

services.label: Services
services.description: What you offer — products, services, packages. Be specific. The agent quotes from this when callers ask "what do you do?" or "do you offer X?".

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.description: How much things cost and what discounts/promotions apply. Include enough detail that the agent can answer pricing questions without escalating.

operations.label: Operations
operations.description: How your business runs day-to-day: hours, locations, service areas, lead times, payment terms. The agent references this for logistical questions.

sales.label: Sales
sales.description: Your qualifying questions, ideal-customer rules, and the workflow for converting a caller into a booked customer.

appointments.label: Appointments
appointments.description: Booking rules: how long appointments take, what info to collect, what days/times are available, when to escalate to a human.

support.label: Support
support.description: How to handle complaints, refunds, and angry customers. Include the boundary where the agent should hand off to a human.

language.label: Language
language.description: Tone, voice, vocabulary preferences. Phrases the agent should always or never use. Languages it should respond to.

compliance.label: Compliance
compliance.description: Legal and regulatory rules the agent must follow — disclosures, recording notices, opt-out language, industry-specific compliance (HIPAA, GDPR, etc.).

knowledgeBase.label: Knowledge Base
knowledgeBase.description: Upload reference documents (PDF, Word, Excel, plain text) that the AI agent can use to answer caller questions. Files are stored on our infrastructure; the agent reads the extracted text on every call.

agentName.label: Agent name
agentName.description: The first name the AI agent uses to introduce itself (e.g. "Hi, this is Sarah from Acme Plumbing"). Optional — leave blank for no persona name.

tagline.label: Tagline
tagline.placeholder: Fast, friendly, fixed right the first time.

shortDescription.label: Short description
shortDescription.placeholder: What the business does in one or two sentences.
shortDescription.description: The agent uses this to answer 'what do you do?'.

elevatorPitch.label: Elevator pitch
elevatorPitch.placeholder: A longer 30-second version. Cover what you do, who for, and what makes you different.
elevatorPitch.description: Used when callers want more detail than the short description.

tone.label: Tone
tone.description: A few adjectives describing how the agent should sound.

voicePreference.label: Voice preference
voicePreference.description: Which agent voice to prefer. The channel can override this.

targetCustomers.label: Target customers
targetCustomers.placeholder: Homeowners in the greater metro area, ages 35-65, who own their home.
targetCustomers.description: Who you serve. Helps the agent qualify leads.

voiceOptions.noneHint: Falls back to the channel default voice.

channels.label: Channels
channels.description: The channels this business serves customers through (calls, web chat, walk-ins, etc.).
channels.empty: No channels yet — add the ones the agent should know about.

fields.name: Name
fields.useCasesPlaceholder: New customer asks for a quote

agentCapabilities.label: Agent capabilities
agentCapabilities.description: What the agent is allowed and able to do (book appointments, quote prices, take messages, etc.).

overageRate.label: Overage rate
overageRate.description: What happens past the included quota — leave blank if N/A.

freeTrial.label: Free trial
freeTrial.description: What trial or free period you offer, if any.

discountPolicy.label: Discount policy
discountPolicy.placeholder: When can the agent offer a discount? Up to how much?
discountPolicy.description: The rules the agent should follow when callers ask for a deal.

timezone.label: Timezone
timezone.description: The timezone the business operates in. Used for hours and bookings.

businessHours.description: When you're open. Mark days closed with the checkbox.

holidays.label: Holidays
holidays.placeholder: Closed Dec 24, Dec 25, Jan 1. Reduced hours on July 4.
holidays.description: Major holidays and any special closures.

afterHoursBehavior.label: After-hours behavior
afterHoursBehavior.placeholder: Take a message, offer to schedule a callback during business hours, escalate emergencies to the on-call number.
afterHoursBehavior.description: What the agent should do when called outside business hours.

qualificationCriteria.label: Qualification criteria
qualificationCriteria.description: What makes a caller a good fit? The agent uses these to decide whether to keep selling or to politely disqualify.

discoveryQuestions.label: Discovery questions
discoveryQuestions.description: Questions the agent should ask early on a sales call.
discoveryQuestions.placeholder: What problem are you trying to solve?

demoFlow.label: Demo flow
demoFlow.placeholder: If the caller is qualified, walk them through: 1) the problem we solve, 2) how it works, 3) pricing, 4) next steps.
demoFlow.description: The structure of a typical sales conversation, top to bottom.

objectionHandling.label: Objection handling
objectionHandling.description: Common objections and how the agent should respond. Key = objection, value = response.
objectionHandling.valuePlaceholder: Acknowledge, then re-anchor on value: explain what's included and the cost of not solving the problem.

defaultDuration.label: Default duration
defaultDuration.description: Standard appointment length when no specific type is requested.

appointmentTypes.label: Appointment types
appointmentTypes.description: The kinds of appointments callers can book.

bookingPolicy.label: Booking policy
bookingPolicy.placeholder: Bookings require name, phone, and email. Minimum 4 hours notice. Confirmations sent by email.
bookingPolicy.description: The rules around how appointments get booked.

cancellationPolicy.label: Cancellation policy
cancellationPolicy.placeholder: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before. Within 24 hours, $50 fee.
cancellationPolicy.description: What happens if a caller wants to cancel or reschedule.

commonIssues.label: Common issues
commonIssues.description: Issues callers ask about often, paired with the right fix.

fields.fixPlaceholder: Direct them to billing@... or offer to email a copy.

escalationRules.label: Escalation rules
escalationRules.description: When the agent should hand off to a human.
escalationRules.placeholder: Caller is frustrated or asks for a manager

supportEmail.label: Support email
supportEmail.description: Where the agent should direct customers for written support.

founderContact.label: Founder contact
founderContact.description: An escalation path of last resort. Used sparingly.

primaryLanguage.label: Primary language
primaryLanguage.description: The default language for agent conversations.

supportedLanguages.label: Supported languages
supportedLanguages.description: Other languages the agent should answer in if asked.

vocabularyPreferences.label: Vocabulary preferences
vocabularyPreferences.description: Words and phrases the agent should prefer to use.

prohibitedLanguage.label: Prohibited language
prohibitedLanguage.description: Words and phrases the agent must never use.

callRecordingConsent.label: Call recording consent
callRecordingConsent.placeholder: This call may be recorded for quality and training purposes.
callRecordingConsent.description: The exact disclosure your agent should read at the start of recorded calls.

dataHandling.label: Data handling
dataHandling.placeholder: We store contact data in the US. We don't share with third parties without consent.
dataHandling.description: How customer data is stored and used. Drives the agent's privacy answers.

phoneCompliance.label: Phone compliance
phoneCompliance.placeholder: TCPA: only outbound-call numbers with prior express consent. Honor opt-outs immediately.
phoneCompliance.description: Telephony rules the agent must follow (TCPA, do-not-call, etc.).

smsCompliance.label: SMS compliance
smsCompliance.placeholder: Include STOP and HELP language in any opt-in. Honor STOP within 24 hours.

ageRestrictions.label: Age restrictions
ageRestrictions.description: Any age gating that applies to your offering.

tenantPrompts.title: Prompts
tenantPrompts.subtitle: Versioned prompt library for all agent roles and channels.

scope.label: Scope
scope.tooltip: Where this prompt applies. PLATFORM = the whole product (admin only). TENANT = your master prompt for the entire business. CHANNEL = overrides for one channel (Widget / Inbound / Outbound). ROLE = overrides for one agent role (Receptionist, Sales, etc.). SESSION = runtime, not editable here.

content.label: Prompt content
content.placeholder: You are a professional receptionist for {business_name}…

tenantBilling.title: Billing
tenantBilling.subtitle: Manage your subscription and plan.
tenantBilling.ltdNote: Lifetime access. The $24.99/mo covers token usage and starts after a 30-day trial. Cancel the recurring anytime — your lifetime access stays.

entitlementTooltips.max_channels: Total number of channels (widget, inbound, outbound) you can have enabled at the same time.
entitlementTooltips.max_agents: Total number of AI agent roles you can configure (Receptionist, Sales, Customer Service, etc.).
entitlementTooltips.minutes_per_month: Voice minutes included per billing period. Overage bills via Stripe at the per-minute rate; calls are not cut off mid-conversation.
entitlementTooltips.widget_enabled: Real-time voice agent embedded on your website. Visitors click to talk.
entitlementTooltips.inbound_enabled: Live phone receptionist. Twilio number routes inbound calls to your AI agent.
entitlementTooltips.outbound_enabled: AI-driven outbound calling for follow-ups, reminders, and campaigns.
entitlementTooltips.affiliate_enabled: Access to the Partner Program — earn commissions by referring new customers.

noSubscription.title: No active subscription
noSubscription.desc: Choose a plan below to get started.

tenantIntegrations.title: Integrations
tenantIntegrations.subtitle: Connect third-party services your agents use to book, call, and communicate.

confirms.disconnectGoogle: Disconnect your Google account? This will disable Gmail and Calendar integrations.
confirms.removeGemini: Remove Gemini API key? Voice sessions will fall back to the platform key if one is configured.

google.title: Google Workspace
google.subtitle: Agent mailbox · Google Calendar
google.notConnectedDesc: Connect the dedicated Google account your agent will use to send emails and manage appointments. This should be a mailbox set up specifically for your agent — not the email address you used to register.
google.needsReauth: Your Google connection needs to be reauthorized. Click Reconnect to fix it.

test.title: Send a test email
test.desc: Verify your agent mailbox can send by dispatching a test message from the connected account.
test.defaultBody: This is a test email sent from your connected Google agent mailbox.

google.footer: Connect a dedicated Google account for your agent — not your personal or registration email. Required scopes: Gmail Send, Gmail Read, Google Calendar. Tokens are encrypted at rest and never displayed.

twilio.title: Twilio (Voice & SMS)
twilio.subtitle: Inbound calls · Outbound calls · SMS — managed by MyOrbisVoice
twilio.intro: You don’t need to create or connect a Twilio account. MyOrbisVoice runs all voice and SMS through our master Twilio account, and we assign phone numbers directly to your tenant from our pool.
twilio.requestPrefix: To request a phone number, go to
twilio.quotaMid: . Your monthly minutes / SMS quota is set by your plan tier — see
twilio.footer: All voice / SMS billing is included in your MyOrbisVoice subscription. No separate Twilio account required.

gemini.title: Gemini Live API
gemini.subtitle: Real-time voice AI · Required for voice sessions
gemini.notConnectedDescPrefix: Enter your Gemini Live API key to enable real-time voice sessions for your agents. Get a Live API-enabled key from
gemini.notConnectedDescSuffix: . Make sure the key has access to the
gemini.footer: API key is encrypted at rest and never displayed. Each account requires its own Gemini Live API key with BidiGenerateContent access enabled.

outlook.name: Outlook Calendar
outlook.desc: Sync appointments with Microsoft 365 and Outlook calendar accounts.

calendly.name: Calendly
calendly.desc: Route bookings through your Calendly event types and availability rules.

calcom.name: Cal.com
calcom.desc: Open-source scheduling — connect self-hosted or Cal.com cloud accounts.

restApi.title: REST API
restApi.subtitle: Connect your own systems via API key
restApi.desc: Generate API keys to push contacts, trigger campaigns, and pull conversation data directly from your CRM, website, or custom tooling.
restApi.footer: Available in a future release. You’ll be able to create scoped keys with read/write permissions per resource.

tenantPhoneNumbers.title: Phone Numbers
tenantPhoneNumbers.subtitle: Search and assign Twilio numbers to your tenant. We handle the Twilio account and billing.

tooltips.getNumberDefault: Search and request a phone number
tooltips.noPlan: Your plan does not include phone numbers. Upgrade to request a number.

tenantPhoneNumbers.planUpgradeCta: Pick a plan to get a phone number →
tenantPhoneNumbers.planUpgradeBody: Your current plan doesn't include any. Upgrade to Basic ($197/mo, 1 number), Pro ($497/mo, 3 numbers), or higher.

complianceBanner.title: About SMS approvals (10DLC):
complianceBanner.body: SMS sending requires a one-time carrier registration we manage on your behalf. Voice + inbound calls work immediately on any number you request. Read the Twilio approval guide →

empty.title: No phone numbers yet.
empty.noPlan: Your current plan does not include phone numbers. Upgrade to request one.
empty.ready: Click "Get a Number" above to search Twilio's inventory and add one to your tenant.

modal.title: Get a Phone Number
modal.subtitle: Search Twilio's inventory. Cost is included in your plan ($1.15/month per number).
modal.noResults: No matching numbers available. Try a different area code or remove the pattern.

confirm.purchase:
Get {number}?

Monthly cost: ${amount} (included in your plan).
confirm.release:
Release {number}?

The number will be returned to Twilio's pool. This cannot be undone.

logo.label: Logo
logo.constraints: PNG, JPG, WebP, or SVG — max 2 MB
logo.errorUnsupportedType: Unsupported type. Use PNG, JPG, WebP, or SVG.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.description: Basic info shown to your team and in communications.

fields.displayNameTooltip: How your business is referred to in conversation. The agent says this when greeting callers ("Thanks for calling Acme Corp…") and when generating email templates. Keep it short and recognizable.
fields.legalNameTooltip: Your full registered business name (with LLC / Inc. / etc.). Used on customer invoices, refund receipts, and Stripe customer records. Optional — if blank, display name is used everywhere.
fields.publicEmailTooltip: The customer-facing email shown to callers when the agent offers to send a follow-up. Different from the notification email — this is what your customers see and reply to.
fields.publicPhoneTooltip: The phone number the agent quotes to callers (e.g. "You can reach us back at this number"). Usually your main business line. Format: +CountryCode followed by digits.
fields.websiteTooltip: Your business website. Mentioned by the agent when callers ask "do you have a site?" or when sending follow-up emails with links. Must include https://.
fields.timezoneTooltip: IANA timezone identifier (e.g. America/New_York, Europe/Madrid). The agent uses this for scheduling appointments and saying "we're open from 9am to 5pm" in the right local time.

industry.title: Industry
industry.description: Select your industry so we can recommend the right campaign templates for your business. If your industry is not listed, select "General" as your industry.
industry.label: Industry vertical
industry.helper: This determines which campaign templates appear in your Campaign Library.

profile.title: Business profile
profile.description: Address and contact details used in confirmations and correspondence.

fields.brandNameTooltip: The short, marketable name (different from legal name) used in agent voice introductions, email signatures, and your website widget. If display name is "Acme Corp", brand name might just be "Acme".
fields.fallbackNotificationEmailTooltip: Where call-ended notifications, missed-call alerts, and other system emails are sent. Used when a tenant member doesn't have a personal notification preference set.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Per-device — enable on each browser you want to receive alerts on.

members.title: Team members
members.description: Invite teammates to share access to this workspace. They must sign up first; then add them here by email.

progress.noQuota: {value} {unit} used — no quota set on your plan

tooltips.sent: Outbound messages your agent or campaigns sent this period. Counts toward your included quota.
tooltips.received: Inbound messages from your contacts. Free — does not count toward your quota.
tooltips.overage: Messages sent above your included monthly quota. Each one is billed on your next invoice at the per-message rate shown above.

voice.heading: Voice — {period}
voice.tooltip: Total minutes across inbound, outbound, and widget calls this billing period. Rounded up to the next minute per call.
voice.rateTooltip: Per-minute rate billed on your next invoice for any call minutes beyond your included quota.

overageSection.title: Projected overage charges this period
overageSection.tooltip: Estimate of overage charges based on usage so far this period. The actual amount finalizes on your next Stripe invoice — usage between now and the period end can change it.
overageSection.disclaimer: Charges are projections based on usage so far this period. Final amounts post on your next invoice.

tenantA2p.title: SMS Compliance (A2P 10DLC)
tenantA2p.subtitle: US carriers require every business sending SMS to register their brand and use case. Fill this in once. We submit to Twilio on your behalf — typically approved within 1-3 business days.

statusPill.submitted: Submitted — Twilio is reviewing (1-3 business days)
statusPill.approved: Approved — your numbers can send SMS at full throughput
statusPill.rejected: Rejected — fix the issues below and resubmit

confirm.submit: Submit to Twilio for review? You won't be able to edit while it's in review.

identity.title: Business identity
identity.legalNameHelp: Must match exactly what's on your EIN registration.

contact.title: Authorized contact
contact.subtitle: The person Twilio reaches out to if they have questions about the registration.

useCase.title: How you'll use SMS
useCase.sampleMessagesHelp: Real examples of messages your AI will send. Include opt-out language ("Reply STOP to unsubscribe") in at least one. Carriers reject vague samples.
useCase.sampleFirstPlaceholder: Hi {{name}}, this is Acme confirming your appointment Tuesday at 2pm. Reply C to confirm, R to reschedule. Reply STOP to opt out.

actions.helper: We'll save the draft, then submit to Twilio. You can come back here to track status.

tooltips.legalName: Must match exactly what's on your EIN registration document. If you registered as 'John Smith DBA Acme Co', use that here — not just 'Acme Co'. Mismatches are the #1 reason carriers reject brand registrations.
tooltips.ein: 9-digit federal Employer Identification Number from the IRS, in XX-XXXXXXX format. Find it on your CP-575 letter or any tax filing. Sole proprietors using their SSN can leave this blank.
tooltips.businessType: Match exactly what you registered with the IRS. Sole Prop = no formal entity (uses your SSN). LLC = limited liability company. Corp = S-corp or C-corp. Non-Profit = 501(c)(3) only. Partnership = LP/LLP.
tooltips.vertical: Pick the closest match to what your business actually does. Carriers reject vague brand registrations — being specific helps. 'Other' is fine if nothing fits, but you may get follow-up questions.
tooltips.useCase: Customer Care has the lowest carrier scrutiny and highest approval rate. Mixed gives flexibility but takes longer to approve. Marketing has the heaviest review. 2FA and Utility are narrow categories — only pick if that's your only use.
tooltips.sampleMessages: Real examples of what the agent will send. Include at least one with 'Reply STOP to unsubscribe' (required by carriers). Use {firstName} for personalization. Don't make these up — they should match your actual flow, because carriers cross-check against your live traffic later.

tenantAgentStudio.title: Agent Studio
tenantAgentStudio.subtitle: Configure your agent's voice and appearance, test them live, then save when you're happy.

testPanel.statusInitial: Select a voice and press Start test
testPanel.greetingThenMic: Agent is greeting you… then press the mic to respond
testPanel.yourTurn: Your turn — press the mic to respond
testPanel.idleHint: Start a test session to hear your agent
testPanel.errorMicDenied: Microphone access denied — allow mic permission and try again
testPanel.errorConnection: Connection to voice gateway failed. Check gateway is running.

widget.avatarDesc: The face your visitors see in the widget before and during the call
widget.voiceDesc: Select the voice your widget agent speaks with

voiceOnly.voiceDesc: Select the voice your {labelLower} speaks with on calls

backToOnboarding.message: When you're done here, click the button to return to the Get Started checklist. Anything you save on this page is auto-detected — you don't have to mark it complete.
backToOnboarding.messageWithMark: When you're done with this step, click the button to mark it complete and return to the Get Started checklist. You don't have to fill out every section — only the parts your business needs.

tenantOnboarding.title: Get started
tenantOnboarding.subtitle: Finish these steps to get your AI receptionist running. {done} of {total} done.
tenantOnboarding.allSetSubtitle: Everything is configured. Your receptionist agent is live.

profile.title: Business Profile
profile.description: Set your business name, hours, and contact details so the agent knows who it is representing.

dna.title: Business DNA

agent.title: Receptionist

channel.title: Inbound Channel

number.title: Phone Number

a2p.title: SMS Compliance
a2p.description: Register your business for A2P 10DLC so your AI agent can send SMS at full carrier throughput. Required if you plan to use SMS.

complete.title: Setup complete — call your number to test
complete.descriptionPrefix: Your AI receptionist is live. Call your number to confirm everything works, then check the conversation in
complete.freeTitle: Setup complete — upgrade to start taking calls
complete.freeDescriptionPrefix: To test the widget, inbound, or outbound calls,

tenantStaff.title: Staff Directory
tenantStaff.subtitleShort: Manage your team and connect their calendars for smart appointment routing.
tenantStaff.subtitleLong: Add team members, connect their calendars, and let the agent route bookings to the right person.

gate.title: Staff Directory is a Premier & Enterprise feature
gate.description: Connect individual team calendars and route appointments to specific staff members automatically.

empty.title: No staff members yet
empty.description: Add your first team member to start routing bookings.

confirms.disconnectCalendar: Disconnect this calendar? Existing bookings are not affected.
confirms.deleteMember: Permanently remove this staff member? This cannot be undone.

tooltips.email: Used for two things: (1) calendar invites for appointments routed to this staff member (Google sends them here), and (2) Google Calendar OAuth identity — the email below MUST match the Google account they connect on the Calendar tab. Mismatch breaks calendar routing.
tooltips.phone: Direct line where the AI agent transfers callers when they ask for this person by name. Format: E.164 (`+15551234567`). Leave blank if you don't want callers transferred to a direct line — the agent will offer to take a message instead.
tooltips.timezone: IANA zone (e.g. `America/New_York`, `Europe/Madrid`). Determines when this staff member is available — the schedule on the Calendar tab interprets day-of-week + time-of-day in THIS zone, not the tenant's zone. Wrong here = wrong booking windows.
tooltips.extension: Optional. If your office has a PBX, the agent will say 'transferring you to extension {N}' and DTMF-dial it after the transfer connects. Leave blank if you don't have an extension system — direct phone above is enough.

calendar.noCalendarConnected: No calendar connected. Bookings cannot be routed to this person until connected.

phone.heading: Phone routing
phone.description: Set a direct number or extension for this staff member. The agent can transfer or connect callers to them by name.
phone.dedicatedTwilioDescription: — assign a specific purchased phone number exclusively to this staff member from your Phone Numbers page. Coming in a future update.

agents.heading: Agent assignment
agents.description: Control which AI agents are permitted to book appointments with or route callers to this staff member.
agents.comingSoonDescription: Currently all active agents with booking capability can route to any active staff member.

adminA2P.title: A2P 10DLC Applications
adminA2P.subtitlePrefix: Every A2P 10DLC registration across the platform. Use the copy buttons to grab field values for manual submission to
adminA2P.subtitleSuffix: until full API automation lands. Mark approved/rejected here when Twilio responds.
adminA2P.platformEmpty: No platform-scope application yet. Use {endpoint} with the same fields as the tenant /a2p form to create one. UI form coming when needed.
adminA2P.tenantEmpty: No tenant has submitted an A2P application yet.

notes.title: Operator notes
notes.subtitle: Friction log captured during manual submission. After ~20 submissions this becomes the design input for the self-service A2P wizard.
notes.empty: No notes yet for this application.
notes.notePlaceholder: What happened? e.g. ‘Tenant left EIN blank — emailed for clarification.’

adminCompCodes.title: Comp Codes
adminCompCodes.subtitle: Generate single-use 100%-off promotion codes for tenants you want to comp at a specific plan tier. Each code is scoped to its tier (a Premier code can only redeem against Premier checkout) and burns on first redemption.

setupBanner.title: Setup required
setupBanner.missingTiers: No comp coupon configured in Stripe for: {tiers}.
setupBanner.instructionsLine: Generators below stay disabled until each tier's coupon exists.
setupBanner.step1Title: 1. Find the 4 product IDs in Stripe
setupBanner.step1Body: Go to Stripe Dashboard → Products. Copy the prod_… ID for Basic, Pro, Premier, and Enterprise (skip LTD).
setupBanner.step2Body: For each of the 4 tiers, create a coupon with: Type = Percentage discount, Percent off = 100, Duration = Forever, Apply to specific products = the matching tier's product. Name it 'Comp - Basic' / 'Comp - Pro' / etc.
setupBanner.step3Body: On each coupon, add a metadata key 'tier' with value BASIC / PRO / PREMIER / ENTERPRISE (uppercase, exact match — this is how this page finds them). Save.
setupBanner.step4Body: The 'Setup required' banner disappears within ~60 seconds (cache TTL). All 4 generators light up. Repeat for any tier still showing as missing.

adminCompCodes.stripeNotConfigured: Stripe is not configured. Set STRIPE_SECRET_KEY in Admin → System Settings first.

generators.title: Generate a comp code
generators.purposePlaceholder: e.g. beta thank-you, early adopter, partnership
generators.disabledForTier: Coupon not configured for this tier
generators.successPrefix: Code generated — send the link + code to your recipient.
generators.successBundleTemplate:
Use this comp code: {code}

Direct checkout link (email pre-filled, code applied): {url}
generators.successNoUrl: No checkout link generated — this tier has no Stripe Payment Link configured. The recipient can still redeem the code at /billing.

directLink.title: Direct checkout link (no comp code)
directLink.subtitle: Pick a tier and a recipient email to generate a Stripe checkout URL with the email pre-filled. Useful for LTD or any tier where the recipient is paying full price.
directLink.noPlans: No plans have a Stripe Payment Link configured yet. Create them in Stripe Dashboard or via the comp-codes setup runbook.

table.emptyFiltered: No comp codes for this tier.

actions.disableConfirm: Disable code {code}? This is permanent and cannot be undone.

tooltips.googleRedirectUri: Leave blank to use the default (https://api.myorbisvoice.com/api/integrations/google/callback). Override only if you've set a different redirect URI in Google Cloud Console. Whatever you put here MUST match exactly what's listed under 'Authorized redirect URIs' for the OAuth client — Google rejects mismatches with no useful error.
tooltips.stripeSecretKey: Must start with `sk_live_` for live mode, `sk_test_` for test mode. Found at dashboard.stripe.com → Developers → API keys → Secret key. NEVER paste a webhook secret (whsec_…) here — that's a separate field below. Wrong-format keys are caught by the validator on save.
tooltips.stripePublishableKey: Must start with `pk_live_` or `pk_test_`. Found right above the Secret Key in the same Stripe Dashboard view. This one is safe to ship to the browser (used by Stripe.js); the secret is server-only.
tooltips.stripeWebhookSecret: Paired with the platform-events Stripe webhook destination (`elegant-wonder` in the Stripe Workbench → Webhooks). Format: `whsec_…`. Each webhook destination has its own signing secret — Connect events use a different secret in the next field. Without this, ALL Stripe webhooks return 400 (signature verification fails).
tooltips.stripeWebhookSecretConnect: Paired with the Connect-events webhook destination (`empowering-dream` in Stripe Workbench). Different from the platform secret above because Stripe locks 'Events from' scope at destination creation — we run two destinations to capture both Account-level events (subscriptions, invoices) and Connect-account events (partner Stripe accounts). Both secrets must be set or Connect onboarding doesn't sync.
tooltips.openAiApiKey: Format: `sk-…`. Found at platform.openai.com → API keys. Used platform-wide for ALL tenants — usage charges on this single account. Updates take effect on the next call without container restart.
tooltips.openAiModelOverride: Default `gpt-4o-mini` is fast and cheap (recommended for production). `gpt-4o` is higher quality but ~10x cost — only worth it if call summaries are visibly poor. Leave blank to keep the current model.
tooltips.geminiApiKey: Format: `AIza…`. Get from aistudio.google.com → Get API Key. ⚠ Free-tier keys without GCP billing enabled expire periodically — enable billing on the Google Cloud project for a durable production key. Used for real-time voice on every channel (widget, inbound, outbound).
tooltips.geminiModelOverride: Default is `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-latest` — the only model that supports the live-audio bidirectional protocol we use. Don't change this unless you're testing a new Gemini Live model. Standard chat models will NOT work here.
tooltips.bunnyStorageRegion: Region code from your Bunny Storage Zone configuration (e.g. `de` for Frankfurt, `ny` for New York, `la` for Los Angeles). ⚠ The Frankfurt (DE) region uses `storage.bunnycdn.com` — the same hostname as NY. Setting region to `de` does NOT change the upload hostname (a common Bunny gotcha). Other regions use prefixed hostnames like `la.storage.bunnycdn.com`.
tooltips.bunnyStorageZone: The Storage Zone Name from bunny.net dashboard → Storage. This is the internal name (e.g. `orbisvoice`), NOT the CDN hostname. Used in the upload URL path. If you rename the zone in Bunny, update here AND ensure the CDN pull zone still points at the right storage zone.
tooltips.bunnyCdnHostname: The Pull Zone hostname from bunny.net → CDN → Pull Zones (e.g. `OrbisVoice.b-cdn.net`). DIFFERENT from the storage zone name above. Used to build public CDN URLs for recordings + transcripts. The pull zone MUST be linked to the storage zone in Bunny dashboard, otherwise CDN URLs return 404 and we fall back to API-proxied audio.
